title: White Gravy (Shahi/Korma Base)
description: A neutral, mildly sweet, rich Mughlai gravy with a signature creamy white-beige color. This luxurious sauce forms the foundation of classic dishes such as Shahi Paneer, Navratan Korma, Chicken Korma, and Malai Kofta.

instruction.title: 1. Prepare Nuts & Seeds
steps: Soak cashews and melon seeds in hot water for 20 minutes.
Drain and blend with a small amount of water into a smooth paste.

instruction.title: 2. Cook the Onions
steps: Heat ghee or oil in a heavy-bottomed pan.
Add cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, and bay leaf.
Sauté until aromatic.
Add sliced onions and cook on medium-low heat until translucent without browning.
Add ginger, garlic, and green chilies.
Cook gently for 2 minutes until fragrant.

instruction.title: 3. Blend the Base
steps: Remove the bay leaf and cinnamon stick if desired.
Transfer the onion mixture to a blender.
Add a little warm water and blend into a smooth paste.

instruction.title: 4. Cook the Gravy
steps: Return the onion paste to the pan.
Add the cashew-melon seed paste.
Cook on low heat for 3–4 minutes, stirring continuously.
Slowly pour in the milk while stirring.
Add water to achieve a medium-thick consistency.
Season with salt and optional sugar.
Simmer gently for 8–10 minutes.

instruction.title: 5. Finish
steps: Reduce heat to low.
Stir in fresh cream.
Simmer briefly without boiling.
For a fine-dining texture, strain through a fine sieve before serving.

chefTips: Keep onions pale and translucent; browning will darken the gravy.
For an ultra-smooth restaurant texture, strain after blending.
A few drops of kewra water or rose water add authentic Mughlai aroma.
Add cream only at the end to prevent splitting.
Store refrigerated for up to 3 days and reheat gently.
Freeze in portions for up to 2 months.
Ideal as a base for Shahi Paneer, Chicken Korma, Malai Kofta, Navratan Korma, and Mughlai vegetable curries.
